_instructions.pdf_support: Place a PDF as 'slides.pdf' in the slides folder for embedded PDF viewer

title: Blink: Modify Code with Example
subtitle: Learn Coding with Arduino LED Blink!
overview: In this session, students learn the basic Arduino Blink program and understand how to modify the code to change LED blinking speed and patterns. Students explore delay timing, digital output, and basic programming logic using Arduino UNO.

youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how to blink an LED using Arduino UNO step by step.

youtubeVideos.title: Understanding delay() in Arduino
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how delay controls LED blinking speed.

activity.title: LED Blink using Arduino UNO
steps: Connect LED positive leg to pin 13 using resistor
Connect LED negative leg to GND
Modify delay timing to change blinking speed

exampleCode.title: Basic Blink Code

modifiedCodeExamples.title: Fast Blinking LED
modifiedCodeExamples.explanation: LED blinks very fast because delay time is reduced to 200 milliseconds.

modifiedCodeExamples.title: Slow Blinking LED
modifiedCodeExamples.explanation: LED blinks slowly because delay time is increased to 2000 milliseconds.

modifiedCodeExamples.title: Double Blink Pattern
modifiedCodeExamples.explanation: LED blinks two times quickly and then waits for 1 second.

quiz.q: Which component glows in the Blink project?
quiz.explanation: LED glows ON and OFF in the Blink project.

quiz.q: Which Arduino pin is commonly used in the Blink example?
quiz.explanation: Pin 13 is commonly used because it has a built-in LED.
OUTPUT means the pin sends signals to components like LEDs.
HIGH means electricity flows and LED turns ON.

quiz.q: If delay time becomes smaller, the LED blinks:
quiz.explanation: Smaller delay means less waiting time, so blinking becomes faster.

quiz.q: Which function runs again and again in Arduino?
What is the purpose of a resistor in LED circuit?
quiz.explanation: Resistor controls excess current and protects the LED.

quiz.q: Which software is used to upload Arduino code?
quiz.explanation: Arduino IDE is used to write and upload code.

quiz.q: What happens when digitalWrite(13, LOW) is used?
quiz.explanation: LOW stops current flow and turns OFF the LED.
